steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to describe how meanders are formed. The correct choice must explain the formation mechanism, not just define, locate, or describe the behavior of meanders.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Over time, many meanders have formed in the Huallaga River, a river in Peru with high sinuosity.: Mentions a specific river with many meanders but says nothing about how they form. ✗
  • Choice B — The U-shaped curves, or meanders, that form in a river will gradually change shape and migrate downstream.: Describes what happens to meanders over time, not how they form. ✗
  • Choice C — A high-sinuosity river, the Huallaga River has many meanders that will change shape and shift downstream over time.: Combines the sinuosity fact with behavior over time, but still skips the formation mechanism. ✗
  • Choice D — Over time, water erodes sediment from one side of a riverbank and redeposits it on the other, resulting in a U-shaped curve, or meander, in the river channel.: Directly explains the cause-and-effect process: erosion on one side, redeposition on the other, producing a meander. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
